title: Bourbon biscuits
subtitle: Dark chocolate sandwich biscuits with chocolate buttercream
excerpt: Bourbon biscuits are a dark, slightly dry chocolate biscuit sandwiched with a thin layer of chocolate buttercream. The characteristic rectangular shape with perforated lines and an embossed pattern is decorative, but the perforations also help the biscuit stay flat during baking.
sourceNotes: Bourbon biscuits were introduced commercially in Britain in 1910 and became one of the most widely sold British biscuits. The name has no connection to bourbon whiskey. The home-baking form (dark chocolate biscuit with cocoa powder and a chocolate buttercream filling) is documented in domestic cookbooks from the mid-twentieth century onward.

recipe.batchNotes: Store unfilled in an airtight tin for 2 weeks. Fill the day before serving.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Unfilled biscuits keep for 2 weeks. Filled biscuits keep for 4–5 days in an airtight tin.

glossaryTerms.definition: A biscuit made from two identically shaped thin biscuits joined with a filling — usually buttercream, ganache, or cream. The filling is piped or spread on one biscuit and the second is pressed on top. The ratio of filling to biscuit is a matter of preference, but the filling should not be visible from the sides before the biscuits are compressed.

content.text: : two thin, dry chocolate biscuits with a chocolate buttercream filling. The biscuits must be completely cold before filling — warm biscuits melt the buttercream and make it weep. Use a ruler to cut rectangles to keep them uniform.
Make dough, chill, roll, and bake

Beat {{unsalted-butter}}, {{soft-brown-sugar}}, and {{golden-syrup}} together until combined. Stir in {{whole-milk}}. Add {{plain-flour}}, {{cocoa-powder}}, and {{bicarbonate-of-soda}} and mix to a firm dough. Wrap and chill for 30 minutes. Heat the oven to 180°C (160°C fan). Roll to 3 mm thick on a lightly floured surface. Cut into rectangles about 3 × 6 cm — use a ruler for straight edges. Prick each biscuit a few times with a fork. Place on lined trays and bake for 12–14 minutes until dry and set. Cool completely.

Beat the filling {{unsalted-butter}}, {{icing-sugar}}, and {{cocoa-powder}} until smooth. Spread a thin layer on the flat side of half the biscuits. Press a second biscuit on top. Leave for 1 hour for the filling to set before stacking.
